shared_groups: Cross-Border Transfer Authorization
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor/personal data/third countries or international organizations; the contract prohibits transfer, which matches the regulation's non-transfer absent authorization
results.condition_check: The contract is stricter: it flatly prohibits transfer to third countries or international organizations, so it necessarily covers the regulation's condition of lacking prior specific or general controller authorization
results.modality_check: 'does not transfer' is a prohibitive restriction, equal-or-greater force than the regulation's 'shall not'
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting timing, manner, target, location, or cause limitations; the contract is at least as strict as the regulation
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data is only processed by the Data Processor at the Data Processor’s address. The Data Processor does not transfer the Personal Data to third countries or international organizations.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: processor shall assist controller with DPIA-related compliance: contract expressly requires processor to assist controller in preparing DPIAs under GDPR Art. 35, which is the same substantive assistance duty and at least as broad as assisting in ensuring compliance with DPIA obligations
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'if assistance is necessary and relevant' is a reasonable qualifier and does not weaken the duty when triggered
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall assist' matches the mandatory regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: contract is equal-or-broader by covering DPIA preparation and prior consultation under Arts. 35 and 36; this does not make it looser than the regulation
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor must ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; contract requires processor ensure its employees processing personal data do so only in accordance with the Instruction
results.condition_check: compatible: both are unconditional
results.modality_check: equal force: 'shall ensure' matches 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: substantively equivalent and at least as strict: 'employees working processing the Personal Data' is a narrower subset of persons acting under authority with access to personal data, but still covers the relevant persons; 'in accordance with the Instruction' matches 'on instructions from the controller'
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall also ensure that the Data Processor’s employees working processing the Personal Data only processes the Personal Data in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: processor obligation to ensure persons under its authority/process personal data only on controller instructions vs processor may only act/process personal data in accordance with documented controller instruction; same substantive instruction-only processing duty
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are unconditional instruction-based limits; contract adds a specific documented instruction and service-purpose instruction, which is not weaker
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'may only' is restrictive and at least as strong as 'shall ensure'
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: no conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause; contract is at least as strict by limiting processing to documented instructions and service purpose
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate effects, and the contract sub-item states a description of the measures the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including measures taken to mitigate adverse effects. This is the same substantive content, and the parent clause makes it part of a mandatory register.
results.condition_check: No condition in either text; compatible.
results.modality_check: The contract sub-item is a mandatory sub-item under a parent 'shall' clause, so it is not merely permissive and is at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner, timing, target, location, or cause constraints; the content is equivalent in substance.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The contract addresses processor processing of personal data and expressly deems post-termination processing to be in accordance with the Instruction; this matches the regulation’s requirement that processing be on documented instructions from the controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract limits the processing to what is necessary and required under applicable law, which is not weaker than the regulation’s documented-instructions condition and is consistent with it.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall' for the relevant processing obligation and the instruction-compliance statement is mandatory in effect.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the regulation requires processing on documented instructions; the contract states that the processing shall be considered in accordance with the Instruction, which substantively carries the same instruction-based control, and the added post-termination timing is narrower/stricter rather than looser.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must return or delete all personal data to the controller after service end; contract requires the Data Processor and Sub-Processors to return the Personal Data to the Data Controller at termination and then delete all Personal Data, with documentation of deletion
results.condition_check: Compatible: regulation triggers after end of services relating to processing; contract triggers at termination of the Data Processor Agreement, which is substantively equivalent
results.modality_check: Compatible: contract uses 'shall' and imposes mandatory return/delete obligations
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: timing is equivalent; contract is at termination, which matches after end of services. Contract is at least as strict because it requires return and deletion, and adds documentation of deletion. The 'provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession' qualifier does not make it looser in substance
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor processing personal data on documented instructions from controller matches the same obligation
results.condition_check: compatible: contract requires processing only in accordance with documented instruction from the controller; this is equivalent and stricter than the regulation's 'on documented instructions'
results.modality_check: compatible: 'may only' is restrictive and at least as strong as 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: purpose limitation to delivering Main Services is an added restriction and does not weaken the documented-instructions requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must inform controller about intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors; contract requires processor to notify controller in writing about identity of a potential sub-processor before engagement. This covers notice of intended sub-processor changes, though the contract is framed as prior notification of identity rather than explicit change notice.
results.condition_check: Compatible: regulation applies in case of general written authorization; contract provides general authorization to engage sub-processors without further specific written authorization, which is the same triggering situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible: regulation requires the processor shall inform; contract imposes a mandatory notification obligation ('shall notify') as a condition to using the authorization.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible or stricter on timing/manner: contract requires written notice before agreements are made and before processing begins, which is at least as protective as informing of intended changes.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: both require the processor to implement/take appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure processing security and comply with GDPR Article 32
results.condition_check: no condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall on contract side matches regulatory shall
results.other_constraints_check: contract is at least as strict by expressly tying measures to the Agreement, Applicable Law, GDPR Article 32; no looser timing/location/manner
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: both require the processor/data processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures; the contract expressly ties this to GDPR Article 32, which is the same security-risk obligation in substance.
results.condition_check: No triggering condition on either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Shall on the contract side matches the regulatory obligation and is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract references Article 32 and applicable law, which is coextensive with the regulation's risk-appropriate security requirement; no looser timing, target, location, or cause constraints.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: processor must ensure authorized persons are bound by confidentiality; contract imposes confidentiality obligation on the processor’s employees, which are authorized persons, and requires strict confidentiality
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Shall on contract side matches regulatory shall and is not weaker
results.other_constraints_check: Strict confidentiality is at least as protective as confidentiality/appropriate statutory obligation; same substantive duty
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s employees shall be subject to an obligation of confidentiality that ensures that the employees shall treat all the Personal Data under this Data Processor Agreement with strict confidentiality.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the contract requires the processor (and sub-processors) to return the personal data to the controller at termination and then delete all personal data, which matches the regulation's processor duty to return or delete all personal data to the controller after services end.
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'at the termination of this Data Processor Agreement' is substantively equivalent to 'after the end of the provision of services relating to processing'; the added proviso that the controller is not already in possession of the data is a narrower/extra condition for return, but deletion is still required.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall return' and 'is hereafter obliged to delete' are mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing is equivalent, and the recipient/object are aligned; requiring documentation for deletion is additional and does not weaken the obligation.
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: The contract requires the processor (and sub-processors) to return the personal data to the controller at termination, and also obliges deletion of all personal data with documentation of deletion. This matches the regulation's processor duty to return or delete all personal data to the controller after services end.
results.condition_check: Compatible: termination of the Data Processor Agreement is substantively the same end-of-services trigger, and the added condition that the controller is not already in possession is a narrower/extra qualifier that does not weaken the duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' and 'is obliged to' are mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'at the termination' is equivalent to 'after the end of the provision of services relating to processing'; the contract is not looser on timing, and it covers return plus deletion, which meets or exceeds the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action align (processor shall assist controller), but the object is different: the contract limits assistance to DPIAs and prior consultation, not ensuring the security of processing
results.condition_check: compatible; 'if necessary and relevant' is a reasonable qualifier and not weaker
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: no timing/location issue; however the manner/object is substantively narrower and addresses different GDPR tasks (Arts. 35/36) rather than security of processing (Art. 32)
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both require the processor to assist the controller with prior consultation of supervisory authorities before processing in the high-risk / DPIA context. The contract also expressly includes Article 36 prior consultation, which is the same underlying obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract's 'if assistance is necessary and relevant' is a reasonable qualifier and not weaker than the regulation's high-risk trigger.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall assist' matches the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'In accordance with GDPR, article 36' and the prior-consultation reference preserve the required timing and subject matter; the contract is at least as specific and not looser.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item states a description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach. Same content obligation, just framed as a register entry within the parent clause.
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause; compatible.
results.modality_check: The sub-item is within a mandatory parent clause ('shall ... include the following'), so the descriptive statement is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is narrower only in vehicle/locus (register entry rather than notification), but the same-information principle applies because the required content is stated here. No incompatible timing, manner, target, location, or cause issues.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap: both concern personal data breach notification content, but the contract sub-item only covers measures taken/proposed to address the breach and mitigate effects; it omits the nature of the breach, DPO contact details, and consequences.
results.condition_check: No condition mismatch; both are unconditional content requirements.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory and the sub-item is a mandatory included item within that register.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location/cause issues; the substantive gap is omission of required notification elements.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: processor/process personal data: regulation permits processing without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law; contract instead requires processing only in accordance with documented instructions, with no legal-law exception
results.condition_check: Not compatible: regulation's trigger is when processing is required by Union or Member State law; contract has no such exception and is unconditional
results.modality_check: Contract is stricter on ordinary processing (may only / only in accordance with instructions), but it does not include the required carve-out permitting processing without documented instructions when law requires it
results.other_constraints_check: Manner differs in a materially stricter way: contract requires documented instructions, whereas regulation allows processing without them in the legal-required scenario
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require the processor to ensure/process personal data only on the controller's instructions; the contract's 'may only act and process ... in accordance with the documented instruction' is substantively the same and at least as strict.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract adds a specific documented-instruction/purpose limitation, which is not weaker than the regulation's instruction-only condition.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'may only' is restrictive and at least as strong as 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's purpose limitation ('only ... for delivering the Main Services') is narrower/stricter than the regulation and does not loosen the instruction-only requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: The contract requires the processor (and sub-processors) to return the personal data to the controller at termination, and also to delete all personal data and provide deletion documentation. This matches the regulation's processor duty to return or delete all personal data to the controller after services end.
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'at the termination of this Data Processor Agreement' is substantively equivalent to 'after the end of the provision of services relating to processing'; the added condition that the controller is not already in possession is narrower/stricter, not looser.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' / 'is hereafter obliged to' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing is equivalent, and the recipient/object are the same. Requiring documentation for deletion is additional and does not weaken the obligation.
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include the nature of the breach, and the contract sub-item (within a mandatory parent clause) states a description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach. This is the same content obligation, not merely adjacent subject matter.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation has no special condition; the contract sub-item adds only an optional 'if possible' qualifier for extra details, which does not weaken the required inclusion of the nature of the breach.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is part of that mandatory register content, so the obligation is at least as strong as required.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract provides the required nature-of-breach content; the additional categories/number details are extra and do not conflict with the regulation's required content.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both impose processor liability/accountability to the controller for sub-processor performance/acts, with the contract phrased as accountability for sub-processors in the same way as for its own acts and omissions, which is substantively coextensive and at least as strict.
results.condition_check: No condition in either text; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory ('shall'); contract uses a plain mandatory statement of accountability, not mere permission, and is at least as strong.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints; 'in the same way as for its own actions and omissions' is compatible with and not weaker than full liability for sub-processor obligations.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align in part: both require the processor to assist the controller, but the contract assistance is limited to handling data-subject rights requests and providing information/documentation, not assistance in ensuring security of processing
results.condition_check: different condition: contract triggers only when a data subject rights request requires assistance; regulation has no such limitation and concerns security of processing
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: timing/manner are not the issue; the substantive object is narrower and different, so the security-processing duty is not covered
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to make available to the controller information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations. The contract instead says the processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided within reasonable time, which is about providing documentation, not necessarily making available the necessary information to the controller for demonstrating Article 28 compliance.
results.condition_check: Compatible; both are unconditional.
results.modality_check: Compatible; 'shall' matches the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible; 'within reasonable time' is not looser than the regulation's timing, which has no stricter timing specified.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided within reasonable time.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: The processor must notify the controller of a personal data breach; the contract requires the Data Processor to give notice to the Data Controller upon a data security breach that can lead to accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to personal data, i.e. a personal data breach.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by awareness/occurrence of a personal data breach; the contract’s definition is substantively the same and not narrower in a way that weakens the duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' is mandatory and 'immediate notice' is at least as strict as 'without undue delay'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'immediate' meets or exceeds 'without undue delay'; no conflicting manner, target, location, or cause constraints.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action align (processor shall assist controller), but the object is different: contract assistance is limited to DPIAs and prior consultation, not responding to data subject rights requests
results.condition_check: compatible qualifier ('if necessary and relevant')
results.modality_check: equal force (shall)
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant mismatch beyond the different assisted task; timing/manner do not cure the object mismatch
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in responding to data subject rights requests; the contract requires the processor to assist the controller when such a request is received and assistance is needed, by providing necessary information and documentation. Same obligation, same actor, same object, and same assistance function.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by a data subject rights request and the need for processor assistance in responding.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall assist' matches the mandatory regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: providing necessary information and documentation is a reasonable means of assistance; 'reasonable time' is not weaker than the regulation and is acceptable.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller: the parent clause states that added sub-processors have been communicated to the Data Controller prior to the relevant sub-processing, which overlaps with the processor informing the controller of intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation applies in case of general written authorization; the contract context concerns added sub-processors being communicated prior to relevant sub-processing, which is not weaker and is substantively aligned with advance notice of changes
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is a plain descriptive statement within a mandatory sub-item context and functions as an obligation; it is not merely permissive
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'prior to the relevant sub-processing' is at least as strict as informing of intended changes, and the subject matter concerns addition of sub-processors, which covers the required change notification
results.contract_source_text: [insert when relevant]

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data on documented instructions from the controller, while the contract requires confidentiality treatment and prohibits processing in conflict with the Instruction unless written agreement; it addresses instruction-compliance only indirectly and not the affirmative documented-instructions obligation
results.condition_check: The contract condition is not the same as the regulation's documented instructions requirement; it allows processing unless conflicting with the Instruction and with written agreement, which is a different framing and does not itself require processing only on documented instructions.
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory, but it is mandatory for confidentiality/non-conflict, not for the specific documented-instructions processing duty.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's reference to 'the Instruction' is not equivalent to 'documented instructions from the controller' as the operative obligation; it is a different and narrower/different-locus control.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor/sub-processors: the contract requires the processor to conclude a written sub-processor agreement and ensure it provides the same data protection obligations as those applicable to the processor, which substantively matches imposing the Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract
results.condition_check: No condition in either text; compatible
results.modality_check: shall / at minimum provide: mandatory and not weaker than the regulation's shall
results.other_constraints_check: by written agreement is equivalent to by way of contract or other legal act; same substantive legal mechanism and no looser timing/manner
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended additions or replacements of sub-processors; the contract requires new sub-processors to be added to a list in an appendix, which is only a list-update obligation and does not itself require informing the controller of intended changes
results.condition_check: The regulation applies in case of general written authorization; the contract applies when the processor initiates sub-processing with a new sub-processor. These are related but the contract does not clearly impose the same notification trigger or the same authorization context.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall' for adding the sub-processor to the list, but that is a different duty from informing the controller.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract addresses list maintenance in an appendix, not the timing/manner of informing the controller about intended changes.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement using the Sub- Processors listed in sub-appendix B. If the Data Processor initiates sub-processing with a new Sub-Processor, such new Sub-Processor shall be added to the list in sub-appendix B under paragraph 2. 7. Remuneration and costs

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, while the contract says the processor complies with applicable law in respect of processing the controller’s personal data; the parent clause only requires providing documentation substantiating compliance after request, not processing on documented instructions.
results.condition_check: Regulation has no extra condition beyond documented instructions; contract/parent condition is a written request for documentation, which is a different trigger.
results.modality_check: The contract’s main clause is a plain compliance statement and the parent clause is an obligation to provide documentation, but neither imposes the specific mandatory processing-on-instructions duty.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation prohibits engaging a sub-processor without prior specific or general written authorization from the controller. The contract requires the processor to conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any sub-processors and to monitor/control their compliance, but it does not require prior authorization from the controller before engaging them.
results.condition_check: The regulation's trigger is lack of prior written authorization; the contract has no corresponding authorization condition.
results.modality_check: The contract imposes mandatory duties, but they are different duties and do not include the required prohibition/authorization constraint.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent prior/specific-or-general written authorization requirement appears; the written-agreement requirement is not the same as controller authorization.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to take all measures required under Article 32 or otherwise ensure security of processing; contract only requires providing documentation of security measures to the controller on written request, which is an information/disclosure duty, not the substantive security-measures obligation
results.condition_check: request by the controller in writing is not the same as the unconditional Article 32/security obligation
results.modality_check: shall is mandatory, but it mandates only documentation provision, not the required security measures
results.other_constraints_check: no equivalent timing/manner/target/location/cause that bridges the substantive gap
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action align on processor assistance to controller, but object/recipient differ: regulation requires assistance in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject; contract requires assistance with data-subject rights requests and providing information/documentation
results.condition_check: different triggering situation (data subject rights request vs personal data breach communication)
results.modality_check: shall on contract is mandatory, but it applies to a different duty
results.other_constraints_check: timing/manner are not the issue; the substantive obligation is different and narrower/different in subject matter
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract parent clause says the register shall at a minimum include a description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach; same content obligation, same subject matter, in-line sub-item within the same document
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory ('shall'); parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is within that mandatory clause, so force is equal-or-greater.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is slightly narrower in phrasing ('likely as well as actually occurred consequences') but substantively covers the required consequences of the breach; no incompatible timing/manner/target/location/cause issues.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: both concern the processor handling data subject rights requests for the controller, but the contract only forwards the request and refrains from direct response; it does not itself undertake to assist the controller in fulfilling its obligation to respond
results.condition_check: compatible: both are triggered by a data subject rights request related to the controller's personal data
results.modality_check: must is mandatory, but the substantive duty is different/narrower than assistance
results.other_constraints_check: immediately is not weaker than the regulation's timing, but timing is irrelevant because the core obligation is not the same
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap: the contract's register sub-item states the nature of the Personal Data Breach, which matches one required element of the notification content, but it does not include the breach consequences, DPO contact details, or mitigation measures required by Art. 33(3).
results.condition_check: No condition mismatch; the contract adds an 'if possible' qualifier for some sub-details, which is not weaker than the regulation's unconditional content requirement for the elements it actually covers.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is part of that mandatory register content, so modality is not weaker for the element it covers.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location/target/cause issues relevant beyond the missing substantive elements.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject. The contract requires the processor to give immediate notice to the controller if a personal data breach occurs. This is related breach-notification, but it does not include assisting the controller in communicating to the data subject, so the core obligation is not the same.
results.condition_check: The contract is triggered by a personal data breach; the regulation concerns assistance in communicating such a breach. The condition is compatible but the substantive duty differs.
results.modality_check: Both use mandatory 'shall', so modality is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract requires immediate notice, which is not the same as communicating to the data subject; it is a different recipient and action. Timing is stricter, but the target/recipient differs materially.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align on one required notification content item: the breach consequences; the contract sub-item states a description of likely and actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach, which matches and exceeds the regulation's required inclusion of the consequences of the breach
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Regulation requires inclusion in the notification; parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is part of that mandatory list, so force is equal-or-greater
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause constraints at issue; content is substantively aligned and not looser
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller/security assistance vs processor providing documentation of its security measures: related to security, but the contract only provides documentation on request and does not expressly undertake to assist the controller in ensuring security of processing
results.condition_check: Compatible in that both involve a controller request, but the contract is narrower and conditional on written request for documentation
results.modality_check: shall matches shall, but the substantive duty is weaker
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location issue; however the object is limited to documentation of the processor’s own security measures rather than assistance in ensuring security of processing
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to contractually impose the Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors, while the contract only says the processor is accountable to the controller for sub-processor acts/omissions
results.condition_check: No condition issue; both are unconditional
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain accountability statement, not an obligation to impose contractual terms on sub-processors
results.other_constraints_check: No matching manner/timing/target/location/cause; the contract lacks the required by-contract-or-other-legal-act imposition on sub-processors
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified content (nature, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures), while the contract only requires providing the register of personal data breaches to the controller/regulator on request.
results.condition_check: Compatible in the sense that both involve breach-related information, but the contract is conditional on a written request and concerns a register, not the notification content.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), but modality does not cure the mismatch in obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is looser/different on object and locus: it provides a register copy on request rather than requiring the notification to contain the required items.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, while the contract instead makes the data controller guarantee lawful processing by the controller itself; different actor and different obligation.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition in either clause.
results.modality_check: The contract is a guarantee/statement, not an obligation on the processor to act on documented instructions.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract’s lawfulness/compliance wording is a different substantive duty and does not address documented instructions or processor processing.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Controller guarantees that the Personal Data transferred to the Data Processor is processed by the Data Controller in accordance with the Applicable Law, including the legislative requirements re lawfulness of processing.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align only partially: both involve the processor processing personal data, but the contract ties processing to post-termination necessity/applicable law and deems it in accordance with the Instruction, rather than requiring processing on documented instructions from the controller
results.condition_check: Regulation requires processing on documented instructions from the controller; contract instead limits processing to up to three months after termination and only as necessary/required under applicable law, with a deeming clause about the Instruction
results.modality_check: Both use mandatory language for the relevant processing clause, but the contract's obligation is not the same obligation and is narrower/different in trigger
results.other_constraints_check: The manner requirement ('on documented instructions from the controller') is not actually imposed; the contract substitutes a post-termination, necessity/applicable-law framework and a deeming provision
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor/sub-processors: the contract requires the processor to conclude written sub-processor agreements and ensure those agreements provide the same data protection obligations as those applicable to the processor, which matches imposing the Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act
results.condition_check: No condition in either text; compatible.
results.modality_check: Shall on the contract side is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's shall.
results.other_constraints_check: By way of a written sub-processor agreement is substantively equivalent to by way of contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law; the contract is not looser on manner.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: both concern the DPA containing contact details, but the contract states contact information for both the Data Processor and the Data Controller is provided in the Main Agreement rather than containing at least one processor's identity and contact details here
results.condition_check: No condition on either side
results.modality_check: Contract is a plain statement, which can satisfy 'shall' if the substance were present
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is a pointer to the Main Agreement, not the actual content in this clause; this is looser because the required processor identity/contact details are not stated in the DPA text itself
results.contract_source_text: The contact information for the Data Processor and the Data Controller is provided in the Main Agreement..

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the breach notification itself to include the DPO's name and contact details, while the contract only requires providing the register of personal data breaches in copy on request; that is a different content/action and does not state the notification contents.
results.condition_check: Compatible only in a loose sense (both can be request-triggered), but the contract condition concerns provision of a register, not breach-notification content.
results.modality_check: Shall is mandatory, but the contract's mandatory provision obligation is about a different object and does not meet the required content obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant overlap on timing/manner/target; the contract is about copying a register to the controller/agency, not including specified items in a breach notification.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to ensure personnel with access to personal data process it only on the controller's instructions; the contract only imposes confidentiality on employees and does not require instruction-only processing.
results.condition_check: No condition issue; both are unconditional.
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory, but it mandates confidentiality rather than instruction-based processing.
results.other_constraints_check: Confidentiality is a different duty from limiting processing to controller instructions; no equivalent timing/manner/target alignment.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s employees shall be subject to an obligation of confidentiality that ensures that the employees shall treat all the Personal Data under this Data Processor Agreement with strict confidentiality.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization, while the contract only requires written sub-processor agreements, equivalent data protection obligations, ongoing monitoring/control, and providing monitoring documentation on request; it does not require advance notice of intended sub-processor changes.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered only in case of general written authorization; the contract has no corresponding notice-on-change condition.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory 'shall' for different duties, but not for the required informing duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing, manner, or recipient for the notice obligation; providing documentation on request is a different duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the breach notification to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate the breach's effects; the contract parent/sub-item requires the processor's breach register to include a description of the measures taken or proposed to address the personal data breach, including measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects. This is the same substantive information, and the contract is at least as specific.
results.condition_check: No material condition mismatch; both are unconditional content requirements for the breach record/notification content.
results.modality_check: The regulation uses 'shall'; the parent clause is mandatory ('shall have and maintain' and 'shall at a minimum include'), so the contract force is equal or stronger.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, location, or cause constraints create a mismatch. The contract's wording is substantively equivalent to the regulation's mitigation-measures item.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for sub-processor performance. The contract requires written sub-processor agreements, flow-down of the same data protection obligations, ongoing monitoring/control of sub-processors, and provision of documentation on request, but it does not state that the processor remains fully liable to the controller for sub-processor obligations.
results.condition_check: No condition in the regulation; the contract adds no relevant condition to liability, but it also does not express the liability duty itself.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall' for its own duties, but not for the specific obligation of remaining fully liable to the controller.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location issue is relevant to the missing liability obligation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the DPA to contain at least one controller's identity and contact details, while the contract only points to contact information for both the Data Processor and the Data Controller being provided in the Main Agreement; this is a pointer to another document and does not itself state the required controller identity/contact details in the DPA.
results.condition_check: No condition issue; both are unconditional.
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain statement, but modality is not the main issue here.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract fails on locus/content: it defers the information to the Main Agreement instead of containing it in the DPA, and it also refers to contact information rather than expressly including the controller's identity and contact details here.
results.contract_source_text: The contact information for the Data Processor and the Data Controller is provided in the Main Agreement..

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: No substantive overlap: the contract text is only a heading/topic label 'Personal Data Breaches' and does not itself require a notification to include the nature, DPO contact details, consequences, or mitigation measures.
results.condition_check: No condition in either text to compare.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory inclusion duty; the contract provides only a bare heading/plain statement with no operative obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause content is provided.
results.contract_source_text: Personal Data Breaches

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not specifically require the processor to take into account the risk of accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to personal data; instead they impose a broader duty to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures under the Agreement and GDPR Article 32
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered when assessing the level of security; the contract does not include this assessment trigger, though it references Article 32 generally
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), but the contract's duty is a different and broader obligation rather than the specific risk-consideration duty
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent timing/manner/target/location/cause for the specific risk-assessment consideration requirement is provided
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must provide documentation/information substantiating compliance to the controller on request, which is substantively the same as making available information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations
results.condition_check: compatible: contract triggers on the controller’s written request; regulation has no narrower condition and request-based availability is consistent with making information available
results.modality_check: equal-or-greater force: 'shall' is mandatory
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: 'after the Data Controller’s written request' is a reasonable request-based timing/trigger and does not weaken the duty
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall after the Data Controller’s written request hereof provide documentation substantiating that:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item states a description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach; same content obligation, just narrower/phrased as a register entry within the parent clause.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are unconditional content requirements for the breach-related record/notification context.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the sub-item is within a mandatory parent clause ('shall have and maintain... shall at a minimum include'), so the descriptive sub-item is mandatory.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract provides the required consequence information and is not looser on timing, manner, target, location, or cause.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the processor must implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure security, which matches the regulation's requirement to have such measures under Art. 32(1); the contract expressly incorporates GDPR article 32
results.condition_check: no specific triggering condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's can/include framing
results.other_constraints_check: the contract is at least as broad as the regulation and expressly references Article 32; no weaker timing/manner/target/location/cause constraints
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the DPA to state the duration of processing, while the contract only states the agreement remains in force until termination of the main agreement; that is the contract term, not the processing duration.
results.condition_check: Compatible in a loose sense (both are duration-related), but not the same condition/obligation.
results.modality_check: Shall on the contract side is mandatory, but it does not impose the required content.
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive match on object/target: contract duration of agreement ≠ duration of processing.
results.contract_source_text: The Data processor Agreement shall remain in force until the Main Agreement is terminated. 10. Termination

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures, while the contract only requires providing documentation of security measures on request; that is an information-sharing duty, not the security-measures implementation duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible in the sense that both involve security measures, but the contract is only triggered on written request and does not cover the implementation obligation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', but it is mandatory only for documentation provision, not for implementing security measures.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is materially narrower: it concerns documentation and a request condition, not the substantive security level/risk-appropriateness requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match the same obligation: the regulation imposes liability on any controller for damage caused by infringing processing, while the contract makes the processor accountable to the controller for sub-processors as for its own acts/omissions; this is a different duty and does not cover GDPR damage liability
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause; not a basis for mismatch
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain mandatory statement, but modality is irrelevant because the substantive obligation is different
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause issue changes the mismatch; 'in the same way as for its own actions and omissions' concerns accountability for sub-processors, not liability for GDPR damage
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specific content (nature of breach, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures). The contract only requires providing the register of personal data breaches to the controller/regulator on written request; that is an access/record-sharing duty, not a duty to include the required notification contents.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / different trigger: the regulation has no request-on-writing condition, while the contract is conditional on a written request.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', but modality does not cure the mismatch in obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object and locus: a register provided in copy is not the same as a breach notification containing the required Art. 33(3) particulars.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: processor must implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure security under GDPR Art. 32; regulation requires such measures and examples include confidentiality, integrity, availability, resilience, recovery, and testing
results.condition_check: No specific condition in either clause; compatible
results.modality_check: Contract uses mandatory 'shall', which meets regulatory requirement
results.other_constraints_check: Reference to measures 'as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including GDPR article 32' is at least as broad as the regulation's security-measures obligation; no looser timing/manner constraints
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that natural persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions. The contract instead addresses the processor's own continued processing of personal data after termination and deems that processing to be in accordance with the Instruction; it does not impose or describe a rule for natural persons acting under the processor's authority or their instruction-bound conduct.
results.condition_check: No matching condition; the regulation has no special condition beyond the general duty, while the contract is limited to post-termination processing necessary and required under applicable law.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for its own processing obligation, but it does not impose the specific instruction-control obligation required by the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and scope are different: the contract is limited to up to three months after termination and backup/continued processing, which is not the same as the ongoing authority-over-persons instruction requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: the regulation requires the processor to return or delete all personal data to the controller after services end, while the contract only annuls the processor's authorization to process data at agreement termination
results.condition_check: compatible in timing/end-of-relationship sense, but the contract condition is about termination of the agreement rather than the end of processing services
results.modality_check: shall is mandatory, but it mandates annulment of authorization, not return/delete of data
results.other_constraints_check: no equivalent return-or-delete obligation, no recipient delivery to controller, and no substantive handling of personal data after termination
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s authorization to process Personal Data on behalf of the Data Controller shall be annulled at the termination of this Data Processor Agreement.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Same actor and general action/object (processor processing personal data), but the contract only states ordinary processing on behalf of the controller in connection with services; it does not address the specific permission to process without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation is conditional on being required by Union or Member State law, while the contract conditions processing on delivery of the Main Services.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'will' for processing, but it lacks the regulatory permission/exception structure and does not authorize processing absent documented instructions.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulatory manner 'without documented instructions' is not present; the contract instead implies processing on behalf of the controller under the service relationship.
results.contract_source_text: In connection with the Data Processor’s delivery of the Main Services to the Data Controller, the Data Processor will process certain categories and types of the Data Controller’s personal data on behalf of the Data Controller.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors; the contract requires documentation of monitoring/control to be provided to the controller on written request, but does not require advance notice of intended sub-processor changes
results.condition_check: Regulation applies in case of general written authorization; contract has no corresponding condition and instead uses a request-triggered disclosure duty
results.modality_check: The contract imposes a mandatory disclosure duty only if requested, but it is not the same duty as informing of intended changes
results.other_constraints_check: Timing/content differ materially: the regulation is about informing of intended changes, while the contract is about providing monitoring documentation upon written request
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement.
